payload.title: South Brooklyn Select Bus Service
payload.publisher: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Proposed SBS for the South Brooklyn east-west corridor identified as a priority service need by the Bus Rapid Transit Phase II Study (2009).
Corridor identified as a priority service need by the Bus Rapid Transit Phase II Study (2009). Serves B82 route with 32,000 daily riders.
B82 Local bus route in South Brooklyn. 22,000 daily local riders.
B82 Limited bus route in South Brooklyn. 10,000 daily limited riders.

tool_args.label: BRT Phase II Study identified South Brooklyn corridor as priority

payload.claim_text: The Bus Rapid Transit Phase II Study (2009) identified the South Brooklyn east-west corridor as a priority service need.
payload.subject: Bus Rapid Transit Phase II Study (2009)
payload.description: The 2009 BRT Phase II Study identified the South Brooklyn east-west corridor as a priority service need.

tool_args.label: Community issues: long and slow trips, underserved areas

payload.claim_text: Key issues raised at public workshops included: Bus trips are long and slow; Many parts of South Brooklyn are underserved by transit; A bus trip across South Brooklyn can take up to 2 hours
payload.description: Key issues raised at public workshops for the South Brooklyn corridor.

payload.claim_text: Long delays at bus stops, traffic signals; Traffic conflicts; Bus stops, access to bus stops need improvement; Crowding during rush hours & before/after school
payload.description: Identified transit concerns on the B82 corridor

payload.claim_text: Delays at particular intersections/bottlenecks; Parking/double parking/delivery conflicts
payload.description: Identified traffic concerns on the B82 corridor

payload.claim_text: High crash locations: Flatlands & Ralph, Flatlands & Rockaway Pkwy, Kings Hwy & Nostrand, Kings Hwy & Ocean Pkwy, Bay Pkwy & 86th St
payload.description: High crash locations identified as safety concerns

payload.claim_text: #1 More reliable/on-time service; Articulated buses; Faster service; More & nicer bus shelters & benches at stops; More frequent night service; Extend B82 to Gateway Center Mall
payload.description: What we've heard on the street - top community feedback items
South Brooklyn Select Bus Service community presentation
The Bus Rapid Transit Phase II Study (2009) identified the South Brooklyn east-west corridor as a priority service need.
Input phase - gather feedback and suggestions from stakeholders and area residents through end of the year (2015)
Dedicated bus lanes as a Select Bus Service feature for faster bus rides and reduced traffic conflicts.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Reduced traffic conflicts between buses and other traffic

payload.description: Signal priority for buses to reduce time stopped at red lights and provide more reliable bus service.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Buses spend less time stopped at red lights

payload.description: Off-board fare collection enabling quicker bus boarding.
All-door boarding allowing buses to spend less time waiting at bus stops.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Buses spend less time waiting at bus stops

payload.description: More attractive, appealing bus stops.
Better trip information for riders to know when the bus is coming.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Better trip information for riders to know when the bus is coming

payload.description: Better visibility for pedestrians, bus operators, and drivers; clearer, shorter pedestrian crossings.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Better visibility for pedestrians, bus operators, and drivers

tool_args.label: South Brooklyn SBS uses South Brooklyn east-west corridor

payload.description: The South Brooklyn SBS project is proposed for the South Brooklyn east-west corridor identified as a priority service need.

payload.description: The South Brooklyn SBS project serves the B82 Local route.

payload.description: The B82 Local route operates on the South Brooklyn east-west corridor.

payload.description: The B82 Limited route operates on the South Brooklyn east-west corridor.

tool_args.label: South Brooklyn SBS has timeline event: presentation

payload.description: The South Brooklyn SBS project had a community presentation on October 14, 2015.

tool_args.label: South Brooklyn SBS has timeline event: BRT Phase II Study

payload.description: The BRT Phase II Study (2009) identified the corridor as a priority, preceding the SBS project.

tool_args.label: South Brooklyn SBS has timeline event: community input phase

payload.description: The South Brooklyn SBS project had a community input phase through end of 2015.
The South Brooklyn SBS presentation was published by the New York City Department of Transportation.
